DISCOVERY 612, PLAINTIFF CAROLINA CASUALTY INSURANCE CO.'S MOTION TO DEEM REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS ADMITTED AND MOTION TO COMPEL ANSWERS TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS ORDERED OFF CALENDAR. THE NOTICE WAS INSUFFICIENT PER C.C.P. SECTIONS 1005 AND 1013. THE MOTION MUST BE FILED AND SERVED 16 COURT DAYS PRIOR TO THE HEARING, PLUS 5 DAYS FOR MAILING. THE MOTION WAS FILED ON JANUARY 23, 2009. THE FIRST DAY THIS MATTER COULD HAVE BEEN HEARD IS FEBRUARY 23, 2009. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.

DISCOVERY 612, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO DEEM REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS ADMITTED AND MOTION TO COMPEL ANSWERS TO FORM INTERROGATORIES IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION HAS BEEN FILED. PLAINTIFF'S REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS ARE DEEMED ADMITTED. DEFENDANTS' FAILURE TO RESPOND WAS A MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. DEFENDANTS ARE ORDERED TO RESPOND TO PLAINTIFF'S FORM INTERROGATORIES WITHIN 15 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED AGAINST DEFENDANTS IN THE AMOUNT OF $215, TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.

Off Calendar May-13-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
PLAINTIFF CAROLINA CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. Moving party is to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =301/PJB

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings;
Off Calendar Apr-27-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Plaintiff Carolina Casualty Insurance Co.'s Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is off calendar as Plaintiff has re-filed the motion to be heard in Law and Motion. The motion for this date was not timely filed and served. Counsel is also advised that no order has been presented for the Order Deeming Requests For Admissions Admitted. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.

Notice And Motion To Deem Requests For Admission Admitted And Motion To Compel Answer To Form Interrogatories; Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-26-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
NO APPEARANCE WAS MADE BY THE PLAINTIFF/MOVING PARTY, CAROLINA CASUALTY INSURANCE CO.
MELISSA EDWARDS, DEFENDANT/OPPOSING PARTY, (415)225-1786

THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Plaintiff's Motion To Deem Requests For Admissions Admitted And Motion To Compel Answers To Form Interrogatories is granted. No opposition has been filed. Plaintiff's Requests For Admissions are deemed admitted. Defendants' failure to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. Defendants are ordered to respond to Plaintiff's form interrogatories within 15 days of notice of this order. Sanctions are awarded against Defendants in the amount of $215, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED. (EAH/612)

ASSIGN TO ARBITRATION CALENDAR LIST
Off Calendar Mar-02-2009 Arbitrator Assigned Directly

Notice And Motion To Deem Requests For Admission Admitted And Motion To Compel Answer To Form Interrogatories; Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-19-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Plaintiff Carolina Casualty Insurance Co.'s Motion To Deem Requests For Admissions Admitted And Motion To Compel Answers To Form Interrogatories, And Request For Sanctions is ordered off calendar. The notice was insufficient per C.C.P. Sections 1005 and 1013. The motion must be filed and served 16 court days prior to the hearing, plus 5 days for mailing. The motion was filed on January 23, 2009. The first day this matter could have been heard is February 23, 2009. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.
